data.body:
Question for players & GMs:

The X-card is a direct, non-verbal way of communicating a single, important piece of information: "Stop everything right now".
There are some other similar safety tools.  The X-Card was expanded to include "N" ('please fade to black'), and "O" ('everyone OK?').
data.author: did:plc:niglajyzldgroh4z3b6x7pqh

data.body:
What sort of things might you want to communicate to a GM in a way which doesn't necessarily interrupt a scene? 
EXAMPLES:
- "Can we take a break after this?"
- "Proceed with Caution"
- "I enthusiastically consent to this, even though my character seems to be hating it"
- "I have a rules question"
data.author: did:plc:niglajyzldgroh4z3b6x7pqh

data.body: Think of it like a semaphore or an ideaogram.  It's a way to communicate a potentially complex idea in a single moment.  What sorts of things do you want to be able to communicate to your GMs?
data.author: did:plc:niglajyzldgroh4z3b6x7pqh

data.body: So…it’s one thing if, as an artist/musician, you want to use AI. I don’t, but do you. I do think it’s weird to suggest that you HAVE to or you’ll be left behind. That framing suggests that a human being cannot come up with any idea that is better than an AI which is trained on…human ideas.
data.author: did:plc:yxfrt7a23thcftnb5ribovjw

data.body: The implied urgency to “keep up” doesn’t make any sense. I’m not racing. Someone “making” something faster doesn’t mean it’s better. I don’t have problems coming up with ideas so I don’t need an LLM to speed up my process.
data.author: did:plc:yxfrt7a23thcftnb5ribovjw

data.body: Crapification isn't design failure. It's the enshittification cycle: platforms prioritize ads and upsells over core function. Winners build owned systems—self-hosted PDFs, local OCR. Dependency on SaaS vendors guarantees this.
